reasoning: The FRED statistical analysis estimated a 69% probability of the 1-year Treasury rate being higher in the short term, due to a recent upward trend (+0.0048 per day over the last 30 days) finishing at 3.98. For the near-term resolution date (June 15), I anchor fairly close to this figure but shave it down slightly to account for potential mean-reversion following the sharp jump from 3.92 to 3.98 on June 6. For longer horizons, macro indications suggest the Fed continues to assess rate cuts through 2025 which could pressure yields downwards over a multi-month period, reducing the probability of the rate staying above the June 8 level. Following the system instructions, my uncertainty increases over time, shifting the predictions conservatively towards 0.5 for the distant resolution dates (July, September, December).
